item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One thing this Tale nails is tension. The prose and pacing are each done well, but you can really feel the urgency. Beautifully done.
This is one of the best tales I've read in a while. The imagery and tone is consistently brilliant throughout, and the length is perfect to get the story across.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This has got to be one of the best new pieces I've read in quite some time. The way the sentences flow together into a coherent whole is like watching liquid gold flow into a solid mass.

Beautiful and terrible with just the right amount of dread.

I can't remember the last time I was so excited about a piece.

+1
You knocked this out of the park. Outstanding first-timer piece that manages to tell an interesting enough story without reading the supporting SCP to stand on its own.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A great first article! Best of luck on your future writings!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't really know what to say except I really liked this. It's pacing was pitch perfect, exactly the right length that I was engaged and entertained consistently. The idea is good, the writing is good. It's just really good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was a fantastic tale. I was entertained the whole way through, the story was compelling, the prose felt natural - overall, I really don't have anything particularly negative to say about this. +1, and congrats on your first article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you so much! Finding a unique way to characterize the Deer as seen by a civilian was probably the biggest challenge, so it's great to hear that came through for you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It was a hundred million people: an infinite audience packed into a single, impossible face. I saw — or imagined — antlers and ice and shimmering metal spheres and, behind that, something more. A warped song, a counter-melody, some sort of response that this thing was preparing, just for me.

It tilted its head, and the entire world bent to match.

Awesome prose, loved how you delve into the eldritch/not quite right nature of the Deer and its conprocs. Heavy +1.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
A love letter to stage fright, unsung containment specialists, and this wonderful imaginative community.

Huge thank-you to critters DianaBerry, Truc Linh, Sonderance, and Tanhony for their input, edits, and greenlights.

Credit to Djoric for creating THE DEER.

+Fun Facts
-Fun Facts
  • Part of a series/canon I'd love to write about the Foundation's HR Department. Natalie Burman will return.
  • I know this might slightly contradict 2845's broken masquerade, and the clause of staff being stuck on-site. There's more lore about that in my mind, and it might show up in another piece someday…but no promises, and feel free to fill in the gaps yourself.
  • Grandpa Eldon, his bari sax, and his draft dodge are real, though Vietnam replaced Korea. He will strike up a conversation with literally anyone.
  • Inspiration for this came from my own experience in the staffing/recruiting industry, which feels like an unexplored narrative in the Foundation with some (literal) killer potential. I haven't found any tales/SCPs that directly involve HR, but if anyone has any suggestions I'd love to read them!

Thank you for reading!
